name: Carbon Footprint Tracker
note: Estimates based on Azure retail pricing. Actual costs depend on usage, region, reserved instances, and enterprise agreements.

services.name: Azure OpenAI
services.purpose: GPT-4o for emission factor mapping, supply chain analysis, natural language sustainability reports

services.name: Azure Monitor
services.purpose: Cloud resource utilization metrics — compute, storage, network for Scope 2/3 cloud emissions

services.name: Cosmos DB
services.purpose: Emission records, supplier carbon data, offset tracking, audit trail, historical baselines

services.name: Event Hubs
services.purpose: Real-time emission event ingestion — energy meters, fleet GPS, manufacturing sensors, cloud usage

enterprise.note: Dynamic PUs, zone redundant, large messages

services.name: Container Apps
services.purpose: Carbon API — emission calculator, report generator, supply chain aggregator, dashboard backend

services.name: Blob Storage
services.purpose: Emission factor databases, supplier documents, audit reports, historical data exports

services.name: Key Vault
services.purpose: API keys, third-party emission API credentials, encryption keys

services.name: Application Insights
services.purpose: Platform performance, emission calculation accuracy, data freshness, API latency tracking

optimization_tips: Use GPT-4o-mini for routine emission calculations and simple reports, reserve GPT-4o for complex supply chain analysis — saves 70% on AI costs
Batch emission events via Event Hubs capture to Blob Storage — process in hourly micro-batches instead of per-event to reduce compute costs
Cache emission factor lookup tables in memory — avoids repeated API calls for standard conversion factors
Use Azure Monitor free metrics tier for basic cloud carbon tracking — custom metrics only for advanced Scope 3 analysis
Archive historical emission data older than 1 year to cool storage — keeps Cosmos DB costs predictable while maintaining audit trail
Set Azure Cost Management budgets with alerts at 50%, 80%, 100%
